title: CachingStore lock discipline (never held across await) is correct and documented
description: CachingStore (cache lib:103-131) acquires the mutex, copies what it needs, drops the lock, THEN makes the network call. A contributor might suggest holding the lock to prevent the thundering-herd problem. Reason to resist: holding an async mutex across a network call (which can take seconds) would serialize all concurrent gets. The thundering-herd behavior (multiple concurrent misses all fetch) is the correct tradeoff: it's bounded by the number of concurrent callers, not by network latency. The lock discipline is explicitly documented in the module-level comment.
